real_questions.text: If fees fall in a bad year, walk us through earnings — which strategies hold up, and is there a fee floor?

strong_answer_markers.text: Anchors the earnings conversation on the durable management fee stream — management fees are the highest-quality earnings in every agency framework — with debt sized against that stream.
Answers the drawdown-year question with the two-part story: strategy diversification plus a fee floor.
For a multi-strat with pass-through economics, presents the pass-through fee structure prominently — it is incredibly credit friendly and can provide rating uplift.
Leads the revenue-mix conversation with the recurring fee-based share and its stability — fee income is stronger credit than transactional or market-sensitive revenue.

agency_intel.text: The agency fee-credit map for performance fees: Fitch discounts them completely (management fees only, ancillary credit); S&P gives 50% credit on a five-year history; Moody's gives 75% credit for performance fees and investment income — most generous of the big three; KBRA gives 100% credit and has no size bias, so KBRA can rate smaller managers investment grade or even A-category. Agency selection materially changes how a performance-fee-heavy earnings profile scores.

For a fee-based manager with little balance sheet risk, the rating is cash flow leverage against fee earnings; for a hedge fund ManCo, the same equation — debt against durable management fee cash flow (2%, or 1-and-20 standard structures), with AUM growth and fund performance as key drivers.
Moody's gives 75% credit for performance fees and investment income — the most generous treatment among the big three.
